text.title: Multi-Region Active-Active Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Multi-Region Active-Active Pattern application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Map data residency requirements per dataset BEFORE topology design. EU PII may NOT replicate to US-east; financial data may require home-region only.
rule.rationale: Topology designed without residency analysis is rebuilt at audit; residency-first prevents.

rule.statement: Each service has explicit RTO (Recovery Time) + RPO (Recovery Point) target. Active-active = RTO seconds + RPO seconds; active-passive = RTO minutes + RPO minutes; backup-only = RTO hours + RPO ≥ 1h.
rule.rationale: Without per-service targets, every service gets gold-plated; cost balloons.

rule.statement: Active-active requires conflict resolution: last-write-wins, vector clocks, CRDT, or app-level merge. Choice documented per data type.
rule.rationale: Conflicts are inevitable in active-active; undefined resolution = silent data loss.

rule.statement: Quarterly failover drill: actually fail a region in production (or staging-with-prod-traffic). Drills that don't fail are theater.
rule.rationale: Untested failover is broken failover; quarterly cadence catches drift before real incident.

rule.statement: Traffic steering policy explicit: geo-DNS, latency-based, weighted, or sticky. Mix of policies = unpredictable behavior.
rule.rationale: Steering policy is the user-visible behavior; ad-hoc mix creates support nightmares.
